title: Pediatric Developmental Screening - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Pediatric Developmental Screening 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S COMMUNITY CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary pediatric care. Services include well baby and child checkups, physical exams, acute care (flu, infection, wound care), chronic care (asthma, allergies, diabetes), pelvic and Pap exams, contraception, sexually transmitted disease screening and treatment, and immunizations. 2252 Lloyd Center Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: Primary care: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 284-5239 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides developmental screening, referrals to educational and community resources, learning opportunities, connects children with other Black children, and supports parents as they help children learn and grow. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 African American/Black children Hours: Varies Email (541) 357-5719 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY CHILDHOOD CENTER CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers services and support to families with children diagnosed with developmental disabilities or experiencing developmental delays. Services include home visits, parent-toddler groups, structured classes, direct therapy, help developing an Individual Family Service Plan (IFSP), and information and connection to community resources. 655 W Fillmore Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Children ages birth to kindergarten-age (0-5) who demonstrate a significant developmental delay in one or more areas and/or meet disability eligibility criteria in one of the following categories: -Communication Disorder -Autism Spectrum Disorder -Hearing Impairment -Visual Impairment -Orthopedic Impairment -Other Health Impairments -Specific Learning Disability -Emotional Disturbance -Intellectual Disability -Deaf Blind -Traumatic Brain Injury Hours: No standard hours (541) 573-6461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES SEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers in home parent education, child developmental screenings and support services. Utilizes the Parents as Teachers (PAT) curriculum 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents age 22 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SCREENING PROJECT DEVELOPMENTAL SCREENING QUESTIONNAIRES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Confidential website where parents and caregivers can check a child’s development by completing an online questionnaire. No personal identifying information is required. Results letters, completed questionnaires, learning activities, follow-up resources, parenting materials, and community referrals are available by email or download. The site also provides contact information for Oregon Early Intervention providers and a Developmental Screening Toolkit for professionals who support families. A project coordinator monitors the site daily and is available to parents, caregivers, and providers by phone or email. Website Eligibility: Parents and primary caregivers of children ages 1 month-6 years Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 346-2580 Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S COMMUNITY CLINIC CHILDREN'S COMMUNITY CLINIC EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM HARNEY COUNTY EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES SEEDS NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER
